controls.label: Does your business have a Beneficial Ownership process?
controls.remediation: Your organisation does not have a Beneficial Ownership process in place. You must establish a documented Beneficial Ownership process to meet your AML/CTF obligations. Review the controls below and implement the required procedures before completing this assessment.

controls.label: Does your AML/CTF program include systems and controls to determine the beneficial owner of each customer and carry out collection and verification either before providing a designated service or as soon as practicable after?
controls.detail-label: Describe your systems and controls for determining beneficial owners and the timing of collection/verification
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification process does not include the required systems and controls to determine beneficial owners and carry out collection and verification activities. Your AML/CTF program must establish appropriate systems and controls to identify beneficial owners and collect/verify their information either before providing a designated service or as soon as practicable afterward, as required under rule 4.12.1.

controls.label: Do you collect each beneficial owner's full name?
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification process does not collect the full name of each beneficial owner. You must collect and take reasonable measures to verify each beneficial owner's full name as required under rule 4.12.1(1)(a).

controls.label: When your customer is an individual, do you assume the customer and beneficial owner are the same (unless you have reasonable grounds to consider otherwise)?
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification process does not apply the available simplification for individual customers. For customers who are individuals, you may assume the customer and beneficial owner are one and the same unless you have reasonable grounds to consider otherwise, as permitted under rule 4.12.2(1).

controls.label: Do you exempt the following customer types from beneficial ownership requirements: companies verified under simplified company verification (4.3.8), trusts verified under simplified trustee verification (4.4.8), Australian Government Entities, and foreign listed public companies (or their majority-owned subsidiaries) subject to transparency disclosure requirements?
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification process does not apply the available exemptions for low-risk entity types. You do not need to apply beneficial ownership requirements to companies verified under simplified procedures (rule 4.3.8), trusts verified under simplified procedures (rule 4.4.8), Australian Government Entities, or foreign listed public companies subject to transparency requirements, as permitted under rules 4.12.2(2)(a) through 4.12.2(2)(e).

controls.label: Does your AML/CTF program include risk-based systems and controls to determine whether any additional information (beyond the minimum requirements) should be collected and verified about beneficial owners?
controls.detail-label: Describe your risk-based approach to determining when additional beneficial owner information should be collected
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification process does not include risk-based systems to assess whether additional information should be collected about beneficial owners. Your AML/CTF program must include appropriate risk-based systems and controls to determine whether information beyond the minimum requirements should be collected and verified for any beneficial owner, as required under rule 4.12.3.

controls.label: Does your AML/CTF program require that verification of beneficial owner information be based on reliable and independent documentation, reliable and independent electronic data, or a combination of both?
controls.detail-label: Describe your verification methods for beneficial owner information
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification process does not require verification based on reliable and independent sources. Verification of beneficial owner information must be based on reliable and independent documentation, reliable and independent electronic data, or a combination of both, as required under rules 4.12.4(1), 4.12.4(2), and 4.12.4(3).

controls.label: Have you confirmed that you will NOT use the safe harbour procedures in 4.12.7 if any beneficial owner is a foreign politically exposed person?
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification process does not properly restrict the use of safe harbour procedures for high-risk individuals. The safe harbour procedures in rule 4.12.7 are not applicable if any beneficial owner is a foreign politically exposed person, as specified in rule 4.12.6.

controls.label: For medium or lower risk beneficial owners, do you collect the information described in paragraph 4.12.1 (full name plus either date of birth or residential address)?
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification process does not collect the minimum required information when using safe harbour procedures. When applying the safe harbour procedure for medium or lower risk beneficial owners, you must collect the information described in rule 4.12.1 (full name plus either date of birth or residential address) for each beneficial owner, as required under rule 4.12.7(1).

controls.label: For documentation-based safe harbour verification, do you verify each beneficial owner's full name and either their full residential address or date of birth (or both) using either: (a) an original or certified copy of a primary photographic ID document, OR (b) an original or certified copy of a primary non-photographic ID document PLUS a secondary ID document?
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification process does not use the correct documentation for safe harbour verification. When using documentation-based safe harbour procedures, you must verify each beneficial owner's full name and either their address or date of birth using either a primary photographic ID or a combination of primary non-photographic ID plus secondary ID, as required under rules 4.12.7(2)(a) and 4.12.7(2)(b).

controls.label: For documentation-based safe harbour verification, do you verify that each document has not expired (except Commonwealth passports expired within the preceding two years)?
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification process does not verify document currency as required for safe harbour procedures. You must verify that each document produced for beneficial owner verification has not expired, with the exception of Commonwealth passports that expired within the preceding two years, as required under rule 4.12.7(3).

controls.label: For electronic-based safe harbour verification, do you verify each beneficial owner's full name and either their full residential address or date of birth (or both) using reliable and independent electronic data from at least two separate data sources?
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification process does not meet the electronic verification requirements for safe harbour procedures. When using electronic-based safe harbour verification, you must verify each beneficial owner's full name and either their address or date of birth using reliable and independent electronic data from at least two separate data sources, as required under rule 4.12.7(4).

controls.label: Does your AML/CTF program include risk-based systems and controls to respond to any discrepancy that arises when verifying beneficial owner information, so that you can be reasonably satisfied that each beneficial owner is the person the customer claims they are?
controls.detail-label: Describe your procedures for responding to discrepancies in beneficial owner verification
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification process does not include procedures for handling verification discrepancies. Your AML/CTF program must include appropriate risk-based systems and controls to respond to any discrepancy that arises during verification so you can determine that you are reasonably satisfied that each beneficial owner is who the customer claims they are, as required under rule 4.12.8.

controls.label: If you cannot ascertain a beneficial owner for a company (not verified under simplified procedures) or partnership, do you identify and take reasonable measures to verify any individual who is entitled (directly or indirectly) to exercise 25% or more of voting rights (including veto power), OR who holds a senior managing official position?
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification process does not include the required fallback procedure for companies and partnerships. When you are unable to ascertain a beneficial owner for a company or partnership, you must identify and take reasonable measures to verify any individual entitled to exercise 25% or more of voting rights or holding a senior managing official position, as required under rules 4.12.9(1)(a) and 4.12.9(1)(b).

controls.label: If you cannot ascertain a beneficial owner for a trust (not verified under simplified procedures), do you identify and take reasonable measures to verify any individual who holds the power to appoint or remove the trustees?
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification process does not include the required fallback procedure for trusts. When you are unable to ascertain a beneficial owner for a trust, you must identify and take reasonable measures to verify any individual who holds the power to appoint or remove the trustees, as required under rule 4.12.9(2).

controls.label: If you cannot ascertain a beneficial owner for an association or registered co-operative, do you identify and take reasonable measures to verify any individual who is entitled (directly or indirectly) to exercise 25% or more of voting rights (including veto power), OR would be entitled to 25% or more of property on dissolution, OR holds a senior managing official position?
controls.remediation: Your Beneficial Ownership Identification process does not include the required fallback procedure for associations and registered co-operatives. When you are unable to ascertain a beneficial owner for an association or registered co-operative, you must identify and take reasonable measures to verify individuals meeting the 25% voting rights threshold, 25% property entitlement on dissolution, or holding senior managing official positions, as required under rules 4.12.9(3)(a), 4.12.9(3)(b), and 4.12.9(3)(c).

groups.title: Process Confirmation
groups.description: Before answering the detailed compliance questions, confirm whether your organisation operates this business process.

groups.title: General Beneficial Ownership Obligation
groups.description: Your AML/CTF program must include systems and controls to determine the beneficial owner of each customer and collect and verify their identity information, either before providing a designated service or as soon as practicable after.

groups.title: Collection of Beneficial Owner Information
groups.description: Minimum information that must be collected about each beneficial owner.

groups.title: Exemptions and Simplifications
groups.description: Circumstances where beneficial ownership requirements may be modified or do not apply.

groups.title: Risk-Based Additional Collection
groups.description: Determine whether additional information beyond the minimum requirements should be collected and verified about beneficial owners.

groups.title: Verification of Beneficial Owner Information
groups.description: Methods and requirements for verifying the information collected about beneficial owners.

groups.title: Safe Harbour Procedures (Medium or Lower Risk)
groups.description: Optional standardized procedures for verifying beneficial owners when the relationship is assessed as medium or lower ML/TF risk. These procedures are not available if any beneficial owner is a foreign politically exposed person.

groups.title: Discrepancy Handling
groups.description: Responding to discrepancies that arise during the verification of beneficial owner information.

groups.title: Fallback Procedure (Unable to Determine Beneficial Owner)
groups.description: When you cannot ascertain a beneficial owner, identify and verify individuals meeting specified control thresholds or holding senior positions.
